# COMMON DISEASE MEDICATION REFERENCE DATASET
# MedRAG Knowledge Base — 2000+ structured entries
# Sources: WHO STG patterns, CDC/AAP/IDSA public guidelines, institutional OPD protocols
# See also: data/medical_protocols/opd_whatsapp_protocols.txt
# NOT for direct patient self-medication


========================================================================
ENTRY_ID: 00001
DISEASE_CONDITION: Urinary Tract Infection - Uncomplicated Cystitis
CATEGORY: Infectious
AGE_GROUP: Infant (1-12 months)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Nitrofurantoin; TMP-SMX (if local resistance low)
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Fosfomycin single dose; Pivmecillinam; Amoxicillin-clavulanate
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Increased fluids; urinary alkalinizer if indicated
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Hydration; voiding hygiene
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Flank pain; fever; male UTI; pregnancy complications; recurrent UTI
CLINICAL_NOTES: Culture-guided therapy preferred; avoid fluoroquinolones first-line when alternatives exist
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Infant formulations liquid/sachet preferred; weight-based dosing; avoid honey <12 months; paracetamol weight-based.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

ENTRY_ID: 00004
DISEASE_CONDITION: Urinary Tract Infection - Uncomplicated Cystitis
CATEGORY: Infectious
AGE_GROUP: Child (1-12 years)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Nitrofurantoin; TMP-SMX (if local resistance low)
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Fosfomycin single dose; Pivmecillinam; Amoxicillin-clavulanate
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Increased fluids; urinary alkalinizer if indicated
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Hydration; voiding hygiene
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Flank pain; fever; male UTI; pregnancy complications; recurrent UTI
CLINICAL_NOTES: Culture-guided therapy preferred; avoid fluoroquinolones first-line when alternatives exist
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Pediatric dosing by weight/BSA; liquid formulations; AAP/CDC pediatric guidelines apply; avoid aspirin <18 viral illness.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

ENTRY_ID: 00016
DISEASE_CONDITION: Urinary Tract Infection - Uncomplicated Cystitis
CATEGORY: Infectious
AGE_GROUP: Elderly (65+ years)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Nitrofurantoin; TMP-SMX (if local resistance low)
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Fosfomycin single dose; Pivmecillinam; Amoxicillin-clavulanate
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Increased fluids; urinary alkalinizer if indicated
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Hydration; voiding hygiene
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Flank pain; fever; male UTI; pregnancy complications; recurrent UTI
CLINICAL_NOTES: Culture-guided therapy preferred; avoid fluoroquinolones first-line when alternatives exist
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Start low go slow; Beers criteria avoid high-risk drugs; renal/hepatic dose adjustment; fall risk sedatives.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

ENTRY_ID: 00019
DISEASE_CONDITION: Urinary Tract Infection - Uncomplicated Cystitis
CATEGORY: Infectious
AGE_GROUP: Pregnancy (pregnant patients)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Nitrofurantoin; TMP-SMX (if local resistance low)
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Fosfomycin single dose; Pivmecillinam; Amoxicillin-clavulanate
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Increased fluids; urinary alkalinizer if indicated
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Hydration; voiding hygiene
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Flank pain; fever; male UTI; pregnancy complications; recurrent UTI
CLINICAL_NOTES: Culture-guided therapy preferred; avoid fluoroquinolones first-line when alternatives exist
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: FDA/category-aware agent selection; avoid teratogens; obstetric co-management; many antibiotics safe penicillin cephalosporin macrolide azithromycin.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

DISEASE_CONDITION: Urinary Tract Infection - Uncomplicated Cystitis
CATEGORY: Infectious
AGE_GROUP: Lactation (breastfeeding patients)

CLINICAL_NOTES: Culture-guided therapy preferred; avoid fluoroquinolones first-line when alternatives exist
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: LactMed compatibility check; prefer agents with low milk transfer; timing doses around feeds if needed.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

========================================================================
ENTRY_ID: 00025
DISEASE_CONDITION: Urinary Tract Infection - Pyelonephritis
CATEGORY: Infectious
AGE_GROUP: Infant (1-12 months)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Ceftriaxone IV/IM then oral step-down; Fluoroquinolone if susceptible
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Piperacillin-tazobactam; Carbapenem if ESBL risk
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: IV fluids; antipyretics
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Hospitalization if severe
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Sepsis; vomiting; pregnancy; failed outpatient therapy
CLINICAL_NOTES: Always obtain urine culture; tailor to susceptibility
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Infant formulations liquid/sachet preferred; weight-based dosing; avoid honey <12 months; paracetamol weight-based.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

CATEGORY: Infectious
AGE_GROUP: Infant (1-12 months)
GENDER: Male
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Ceftriaxone IV/IM then oral step-down; Fluoroquinolone if susceptible
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Piperacillin-tazobactam; Carbapenem if ESBL risk
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: IV fluids; antipyretics
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Hospitalization if severe
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Sepsis; vomiting; pregnancy; failed outpatient therapy
CLINICAL_NOTES: Always obtain urine culture; tailor to susceptibility
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Infant formulations liquid/sachet preferred; weight-based dosing; avoid honey <12 months; paracetamol weight-based.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Fluid replacement; continue feeding children

AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Start low go slow; Beers criteria avoid high-risk drugs; renal/hepatic dose adjustment; fall risk sedatives.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Salbutamol trial only if wheeze; nebulized hypertonic saline selected
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Nasal suction; hydration
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Oxygen if needed
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Apnea; poor feeding; SpO2 <90%
CLINICAL_NOTES: CDC: antibiotics not indicated for bronchiolitis
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Pediatric dosing by weight/BSA; liquid formulations; AAP/CDC pediatric guidelines apply; avoid aspirin <18 viral illness.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: ICS-LABA; Leukotriene antagonist step 2+
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Reliever SABA or ICS-formoterol MART
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Trigger avoidance; inhaler technique
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Status asthmaticus; frequent oral steroid bursts
CLINICAL_NOTES: GINA: ICS preferred over montelukast first-line
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Start low go slow; Beers criteria avoid high-risk drugs; renal/hepatic dose adjustment; fall risk sedatives.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

CATEGORY: Cardiovascular
AGE_GROUP: Infant (1-12 months)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Thiazide diuretic; ACE inhibitor; ARB; CCB
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Combination therapy if needed
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: DASH diet; sodium restriction; exercise
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Lifestyle modification first
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Hypertensive emergency; secondary causes
CLINICAL_NOTES: Amlodipine common in OPD protocols
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Infant formulations liquid/sachet preferred; weight-based dosing; avoid honey <12 months; paracetamol weight-based.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.

ENTRY_ID: 00427
DISEASE_CONDITION: Hyperthyroidism - Graves
CATEGORY: Endocrine
AGE_GROUP: Infant (1-12 months)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Methimazole; Propylthiouracil 1st trimester pregnancy
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Per local formulary and specialist guidance
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Propranolol symptomatic; Radioiodine/surgery definitive
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Avoid iodine excess

DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

DISEASE_CONDITION: GERD
CATEGORY: Gastrointestinal
AGE_GROUP: Elderly (65+ years)
GENDER: Female
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: PPI; Lifestyle elevation head of bed
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: H2 blocker; Alginate
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Weight loss; avoid late meals
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Dietary triggers avoidance
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Dysphagia; odynophagia; GI bleed
CLINICAL_NOTES: Step-down PPI when controlled
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Start low go slow; Beers criteria avoid high-risk drugs; renal/hepatic dose adjustment; fall risk sedatives.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Liver cancer surveillance
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Decompensated cirrhosis
CLINICAL_NOTES: Long-term antiviral often required
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: FDA/category-aware agent selection; avoid teratogens; obstetric co-management; many antibiotics safe penicillin cephalosporin macrolide azithromycin.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.

AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Infant formulations liquid/sachet preferred; weight-based dosing; avoid honey <12 months; paracetamol weight-based.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

DISEASE_CONDITION: Acne Vulgaris - Mild
CATEGORY: Dermatology
AGE_GROUP: Pregnancy (pregnant patients)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Topical retinoid; Benzoyl peroxide
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Topical antibiotic combination
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Gentle cleanser
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Avoid picking
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Nodulocystic; scarring
CLINICAL_NOTES: Limit antibiotic monotherapy duration
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: FDA/category-aware agent selection; avoid teratogens; obstetric co-management; many antibiotics safe penicillin cephalosporin macrolide azithromycin.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

ENTRY_ID: 01003
DISEASE_CONDITION: Allergic Rhinitis
CATEGORY: ENT/Allergy
AGE_GROUP: Infant (1-12 months)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Intranasal corticosteroid
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Oral antihistamine; Montelukast add-on
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Allergen avoidance; saline rinse
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Environmental control
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Asthma comorbidity
CLINICAL_NOTES: Montelukast-levoce tirizine in OPD URTI
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Infant formulations liquid/sachet preferred; weight-based dosing; avoid honey <12 months; paracetamol weight-based.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

DISEASE_CONDITION: Conjunctivitis - Bacterial
CATEGORY: Ophthalmology
AGE_GROUP: Young_Adult (18-39 years)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Topical antibiotic drops
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Per local formulary and specialist guidance
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Hygiene; avoid contact lenses
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Warm compress
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Neonatal; herpes; trauma
CLINICAL_NOTES: Viral more common — antibiotics not always needed
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Standard adult dosing; pregnancy test if relevant; drug interactions with OCP consider.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

AGE_GROUP: Infant (1-12 months)
GENDER: Male
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Oral ferrous sulfate
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: IV iron if intolerance/malabsorption
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Treat underlying cause
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Dietary iron
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: GI malignancy workup adults; transfusion if severe
CLINICAL_NOTES: Continue 3 months after Hb normalized
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Infant formulations liquid/sachet preferred; weight-based dosing; avoid honey <12 months; paracetamol weight-based.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: ACT (artemether-lumefantrine)
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Atovaquone-proguanil; Quinine + doxycycline
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Supportive; hydration
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Bed nets; prophylaxis travelers
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Severe malaria criteria
CLINICAL_NOTES: Resistance patterns regional
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: FDA/category-aware agent selection; avoid teratogens; obstetric co-management; many antibiotics safe penicillin cephalosporin macrolide azithromycin.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Adherence counseling
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Opportunistic infection acute
CLINICAL_NOTES: Start ART same day if ready
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Standard adult dosing; monitor cardiovascular renal risk; polypharmacy interactions.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

CLINICAL_NOTES: Penicillin preferred
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: LactMed compatibility check; prefer agents with low milk transfer; timing doses around feeds if needed.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Oral ivermectin if crusted
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Treat contacts; wash bedding
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Pruritus may persist weeks
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Crusted scabies; institutional outbreak
CLINICAL_NOTES: Repeat application per protocol
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Start low go slow; Beers criteria avoid high-risk drugs; renal/hepatic dose adjustment; fall risk sedatives.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

ENTRY_ID: 01363
DISEASE_CONDITION: Chickenpox - Uncomplicated
CATEGORY: Infectious
AGE_GROUP: Infant (1-12 months)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Supportive; acyclovir if high-risk
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Paracetamol; avoid aspirin children
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Isolation
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Immunocompromised; pregnancy
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Varicella pneumonia; encephalitis
CLINICAL_NOTES: VZV vaccine prevention
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Infant formulations liquid/sachet preferred; weight-based dosing; avoid honey <12 months; paracetamol weight-based.

CATEGORY: Infectious
AGE_GROUP: Infant (1-12 months)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Acyclovir/valacyclovir within 72h

REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Post-herpetic neuralgia

FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Oseltamivir if within 48h high-risk
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Supportive; paracetamol
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Rest; hydration
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Vaccination prevention
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Pneumonia; respiratory failure
CLINICAL_NOTES: Neuraminidase inhibitors per CDC
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Start low go slow; Beers criteria avoid high-risk drugs; renal/hepatic dose adjustment; fall risk sedatives.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: LactMed compatibility check; prefer agents with low milk transfer; timing doses around feeds if needed.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

AGE_GROUP: Child (1-12 years)
GENDER: Female
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Supportive: analgesia fluids
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: No antibiotics
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Rest; salt gargle
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Strep test before antibiotics
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Peritonsillar abscess
CLINICAL_NOTES: Most sore throats viral
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Pediatric dosing by weight/BSA; liquid formulations; AAP/CDC pediatric guidelines apply; avoid aspirin <18 viral illness.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

CATEGORY: Pediatric
AGE_GROUP: Elderly (65+ years)
GENDER: Female
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Cholecalciferol supplementation
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Per local formulary and specialist guidance
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Calcium if deficient; Sun exposure safe
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Treat underlying malabsorption
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Deformity severe
CLINICAL_NOTES: Alkaline phosphatase elevated
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Start low go slow; Beers criteria avoid high-risk drugs; renal/hepatic dose adjustment; fall risk sedatives.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

ENTRY_ID: 01857
DISEASE_CONDITION: Anaphylaxis
CATEGORY: Emergency/Allergy
AGE_GROUP: Infant (1-12 months)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: IM epinephrine first-line
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Adjunct antihistamine; steroid; bronchodilator
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Lie flat legs elevated unless breathing difficulty
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Observation biphasic reaction
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Airway compromise; hypotension
CLINICAL_NOTES: Carry auto-injector education
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Infant formulations liquid/sachet preferred; weight-based dosing; avoid honey <12 months; paracetamol weight-based.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

AGE_GROUP: Elderly (65+ years)
GENDER: Male
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Oral potassium chloride
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: IV potassium if severe/symptomatic
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Correct magnesium
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Arrhythmia; weakness severe
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Max IV rate limits
CLINICAL_NOTES: Find cause diuretics diarrhea
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Start low go slow; Beers criteria avoid high-risk drugs; renal/hepatic dose adjustment; fall risk sedatives.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Altered mental status
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Seizure; unconscious
CLINICAL_NOTES: Rule out sulfonylurea overdose

GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Standard adult dosing; pregnancy test if relevant; drug interactions with OCP consider.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.

DISEASE_CONDITION: Hyperlipidemia - Primary Prevention
CATEGORY: Cardiovascular
AGE_GROUP: Elderly (65+ years)
GENDER: Female
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Statin moderate intensity
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Ezetimibe add-on; PCSK9i if very high risk
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Mediterranean diet; exercise
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: ASCVD risk calculator guides
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Statin intolerance; familial hypercholesterolemia
CLINICAL_NOTES: Lifestyle always adjunct
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Start low go slow; Beers criteria avoid high-risk drugs; renal/hepatic dose adjustment; fall risk sedatives.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

ENTRY_ID: 02235
DISEASE_CONDITION: Hyperlipidemia - Primary Prevention
CATEGORY: Cardiovascular
AGE_GROUP: Pregnancy (pregnant patients)
GENDER: All

GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

ENTRY_ID: 02241
DISEASE_CONDITION: Thyroid Nodule - Euthyroid
CATEGORY: Endocrine
AGE_GROUP: Infant (1-12 months)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: TSH; Ultrasound; FNA if indicated
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Levothyroxine if hypothyroid
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Surveillance by size/features
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Compression symptoms; suspicious FNA
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Surgery malignant cytology
CLINICAL_NOTES: Do not treat euthyroid nodule with LT4 routinely
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Infant formulations liquid/sachet preferred; weight-based dosing; avoid honey <12 months; paracetamol weight-based.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

========================================================================
ENTRY_ID: 02264
DISEASE_CONDITION: Thyroid Nodule - Euthyroid
CATEGORY: Endocrine
AGE_GROUP: Lactation (breastfeeding patients)
GENDER: Male
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: TSH; Ultrasound; FNA if indicated
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Levothyroxine if hypothyroid
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Surveillance by size/features
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Compression symptoms; suspicious FNA
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Surgery malignant cytology
CLINICAL_NOTES: Do not treat euthyroid nodule with LT4 routinely
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: LactMed compatibility check; prefer agents with low milk transfer; timing doses around feeds if needed.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

AGE_GROUP: Middle_Adult (40-64 years)
GENDER: Male
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Ciprofloxacin; Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Analgesia; Alpha-blocker
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Hydration
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Urinary retention; sepsis
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: 4 weeks treatment often needed
CLINICAL_NOTES: Chronic prostatitis different approach
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Standard adult dosing; monitor cardiovascular renal risk; polypharmacy interactions.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

CATEGORY: Nephrology
AGE_GROUP: Pregnancy (pregnant patients)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: ACEi/ARB if proteinuria; BP control
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: SGLT2i if diabetic; Phosphate binder late
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Nephrology co-care
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Avoid nephrotoxins NSAIDs
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Hyperkalemia; rapid progression
CLINICAL_NOTES: Individualize BP targets
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: FDA/category-aware agent selection; avoid teratogens; obstetric co-management; many antibiotics safe penicillin cephalosporin macrolide azithromycin.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Bleeding; encephalopathy; HCC
CLINICAL_NOTES: Avoid sedatives NSAIDs

ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: HCC surveillance 6-month ultrasound

CLINICAL_NOTES: Avoid sedatives NSAIDs

DISEASE_CONDITION: Celiac Disease
CATEGORY: Gastrointestinal
AGE_GROUP: Pregnancy (pregnant patients)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Strict lifelong gluten-free diet
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Supplement iron B12 D if deficient
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Dietitian referral
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Osteoporosis screening
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Refractory celiac; lymphoma risk
CLINICAL_NOTES: Serology biopsy diagnosis
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: FDA/category-aware agent selection; avoid teratogens; obstetric co-management; many antibiotics safe penicillin cephalosporin macrolide azithromycin.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

DISEASE_CONDITION: Psoriasis - Plaque Moderate
CATEGORY: Dermatology
AGE_GROUP: Young_Adult (18-39 years)
GENDER: Male
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Topical vitamin D analog + steroid
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Phototherapy; Methotrexate; Biologic severe
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Emollient; Smoking cessation
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Psoriatic arthritis screen
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Erythroderma; pustular emergency
CLINICAL_NOTES: Stepwise by BSA
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Standard adult dosing; pregnancy test if relevant; drug interactions with OCP consider.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

ENTRY_ID: 02917
DISEASE_CONDITION: Rosacea
CATEGORY: Dermatology
AGE_GROUP: Infant (1-12 months)
GENDER: All

ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Oral doxycycline anti-inflammatory dose
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Trigger avoidance alcohol heat
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Ocular rosacea
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Rhinophyma severe
CLINICAL_NOTES: Gentle skin care
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Infant formulations liquid/sachet preferred; weight-based dosing; avoid honey <12 months; paracetamol weight-based.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Sunscreen strict; Topical hydroquinone/tretinoin
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Azelaic acid; Chemical peel specialist
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Hormonal trigger OCP review
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Exclude melasma mimics
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Cosmetic counseling
CLINICAL_NOTES: Sun protection essential
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: FDA/category-aware agent selection; avoid teratogens; obstetric co-management; many antibiotics safe penicillin cephalosporin macrolide azithromycin.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Diabetes immunocompromise
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Spreading abscess
CLINICAL_NOTES: Do not squeeze
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: FDA/category-aware agent selection; avoid teratogens; obstetric co-management; many antibiotics safe penicillin cephalosporin macrolide azithromycin.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

CATEGORY: Infectious
AGE_GROUP: Middle_Adult (40-64 years)
GENDER: Male
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Doxycycline mild; Penicillin/ceftriaxone severe
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Supportive
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Exposure freshwater animals
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Weil disease ICU
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Jaundice renal failure
CLINICAL_NOTES: Endemic areas occupational
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Standard adult dosing; monitor cardiovascular renal risk; polypharmacy interactions.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Doxycycline + rifampin or aminoglycoside
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Prolonged course weeks
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Animal exposure history
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Endocarditis osteoarticular
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Culture serology
CLINICAL_NOTES: Combination therapy standard
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: LactMed compatibility check; prefer agents with low milk transfer; timing doses around feeds if needed.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Screen other STIs
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Pregnancy metronidazole safe
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Persistent reinfection
CLINICAL_NOTES: Both partners treat
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Pediatric dosing by weight/BSA; liquid formulations; AAP/CDC pediatric guidelines apply; avoid aspirin <18 viral illness.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

AGE_GROUP: Elderly (65+ years)
GENDER: Male
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Supportive; Heart failure therapy
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Avoid strenuous activity 3-6 months
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Arrhythmia monitoring
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Cardiogenic shock
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Viral common cause
CLINICAL_NOTES: Cardiology admission if severe
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Start low go slow; Beers criteria avoid high-risk drugs; renal/hepatic dose adjustment; fall risk sedatives.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

CATEGORY: Cardiovascular

DISEASE_CONDITION: Sick Sinus Syndrome
CATEGORY: Cardiology
AGE_GROUP: Pregnancy (pregnant patients)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Pacemaker if symptomatic bradycardia
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Review medications beta-blocker
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Syncope falls elderly
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Tachy-brady syndrome
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Electrophysiology
CLINICAL_NOTES: Anticoagulation if AF coexists
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: FDA/category-aware agent selection; avoid teratogens; obstetric co-management; many antibiotics safe penicillin cephalosporin macrolide azithromycin.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

ENTRY_ID: 03823
DISEASE_CONDITION: Wolff-Parkinson-White
CATEGORY: Cardiology
AGE_GROUP: Infant (1-12 months)
GENDER: All
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Procainamide/flecainide stable narrow complex
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Avoid AV nodal blocker alone pre-excited AF
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Catheter ablation definitive
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Orthodromic AVRT
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Antidromic AF dangerous
CLINICAL_NOTES: Cardiology electrophysiology
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Infant formulations liquid/sachet preferred; weight-based dosing; avoid honey <12 months; paracetamol weight-based.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Individualize for patient comorbidities allergies renal hepatic function.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

CATEGORY: Cardiology
AGE_GROUP: Infant (1-12 months)
GENDER: Male
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: Beta-blocker nadolol/propranolol
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Avoid QT-prolonging drugs list
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: ICD high-risk
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Syncope family history sudden death
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Electrolyte K Mg Ca correct
CLINICAL_NOTES: Genetic testing family

GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

GENDER: Male
FIRST_LINE_MEDICATIONS: GDMT heart failure ACEi beta-blocker MRA SGLT2i
ALTERNATIVE_MEDICATIONS: Device CRT ICD if criteria
ADJUNCT_SUPPORTIVE: Alcohol cessation
NON_PHARMACOLOGICAL: Arrhythmia thromboembolism
REFER_URGENT_WHEN: Transplant end-stage
CLINICAL_NOTES: Treat reversible causes
AGE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: FDA/category-aware agent selection; avoid teratogens; obstetric co-management; many antibiotics safe penicillin cephalosporin macrolide azithromycin.
GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider BPH alpha-blocker interactions; erectile dysfunction drug interactions; prostate screening age-appropriate.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.

GENDER_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE: Consider pregnancy/lactation potential; gynecologic drug interactions; iron needs menstruation.
DISCLAIMER: Educational protocol reference only. Licensed clinician must confirm diagnosis, dosing, allergies, pregnancy status, and local resistance patterns before prescribing.
